mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recessionary pressures, causing investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns during mid-cycle expansion phases (hit rate < 40%).

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 220.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations within the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE spread shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and earnings, establishing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates exhibit zero correlation with 60-day S&P 500 returns (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index below 90.0 signals severe supply-side contraction and capacity underutilization, triggering recession fears and forcing equity portfolios to price in negative earnings over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO falls below 90.0.
